topic: Why sleeping less than 6 hours raises cancer risk
caption: Did you know sleeping less than 6 hours a night can increase your cancer risk by 20%? 🚨 Chronic sleep deprivation disrupts your body's natural repair processes, including DNA repair. 💡 This can lead to genetic mutations that may cause cancer. 🌃 Furthermore, sleep affects the immune system, which is crucial for fighting cancer cells. 🧬 Research shows that adults who sleep less than 6 hours per night have a higher risk of developing colon, breast, and prostate cancers. 📊 Save this so you don't lose it. Share with someone who needs to see this. Follow @healthrecode for daily science-backed health. Don't forget — your future body will thank you. 💤 #SleepAndCancer #CancerPrevention #SleepIsKey #HealthRecode #CancerRisk #SleepDeprivation #ImmuneSystem #DNARepair
